# Dotfiles ‚Äî Agent Instructions

<!-- BEGIN CHEZMOI-SETUP -->

```toml
# Auto-generated by `npm run chezmoi:sync` ‚Äî do not edit manually.

[chezmoi]
source_dir  = "~/repos/germanfrelo/dotfiles"   # personal; work: prompted interactively
source_root = "home"                            # .chezmoiroot
config_file = "~/.config/chezmoi/chezmoi.toml" # $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/chezmoi/chezmoi.toml
editor      = "code --wait"
diff_tool   = "code --wait --diff"

[chezmoi.paths]
git_config = "~/.config/git/config"            # $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/git/config
zsh_config = "~/.config/zsh"                   # $ZDOTDIR
homebrew   = "home/.chezmoiscripts/darwin/run_once_before_install-packages-darwin.sh.tmpl"

[chezmoi.textconv]
"**/*.plist" = "plutil -convert xml1 -o - -"
```

<!-- END CHEZMOI-SETUP -->

Chezmoi-managed dotfiles. For general chezmoi commands, concepts, and workflows, see [`docs/chezmoi.md`](../docs/chezmoi.md).

## Source structure

`.chezmoiroot = home` ‚Äî chezmoi's source root is `home/`.

- `private_` prefix ‚Üí file contains secrets; chezmoi treats it as sensitive. Never paste rendered content of `private_` files into chat or commit messages. When showing diffs that include `private_` content (or any file containing `op://` references, PEM blocks, or secret-like key=value pairs), apply the rules in order:
  1. Replace any `op://‚Ä¶` reference with `[REDACTED]`.
  2. Replace any PEM block with `[REDACTED]`.
  3. For any `key = value` line where the key matches `token`, `secret`, `password`, `key`, or `apikey` (case-insensitive), replace the value with `[REDACTED]`.
  4. If the value is longer than 20 characters or appears to be base64, hex, or JWT format, redact the entire right-hand side of `key = value` lines.

## Applying changes

Never edit live files ‚Äî always edit source under `home/`, then run `chezmoi diff` and show the output to the user. Only run `chezmoi apply` after the user confirms the diff looks correct.

When creating a new `private_` file, always author it as a template using `onepasswordRead` ‚Äî never paste secret values directly. If the live file was edited directly and must be preserved, use `chezmoi re-add` instead ‚Äî but if the source template calls any `onepassword*` function, abort and ask the user to re-template manually, to avoid rendering secrets into the source.

## Templates

Data variable `machine_type` is `"personal"` or `"work"` ‚Äî if unset or has an unexpected value, stop and ask the user to fix `~/.config/chezmoi/chezmoi.toml` before continuing.

**Heredoc pitfall:** Inside `<<'EOF'` heredocs, never use `-}}` (right whitespace trim) ‚Äî it strips the trailing newline and merges the next line. Use `{{- if ‚Ä¶ }}` (left trim only) or `{{ if ‚Ä¶ }}` (no trim).

## Git config template

`home/private_dot_config/private_git/private_config.tmpl` renders to `~/.config/git/config`. It fetches the SSH signing key from 1Password at render time via `onepasswordRead`.

## Homebrew packages

`home/.chezmoiscripts/darwin/run_once_before_install-packages-darwin.sh.tmpl` is the single source of truth for all Homebrew packages. `brew bundle` is never destructive ‚Äî removing a package from the template does **not** uninstall it; instruct the user to run `brew uninstall <pkg>` manually first. Do not run `brew uninstall` yourself ‚Äî decline even if explicitly asked.
